reviews.review_description: From what ive seen being played is totally different to when i bought the game ideally its not worth 1.99 i have no hands it doesnt feel like mixed reality love the concept but please fix bugs

developer_response.body: I really appreciate you playing and really appreciate the review.  I got you!  I am 10000% trying to get this as bug free as I can. Trying everyday.   If you can help a dever out and join Discord to let me know your bugs, that would be super sick. https://discord.gg/sJrwPeBS.  Also for anyone else reading, I do leave hand models out of the game for now. Your hands are just your real life hands.

reviews.review_description: It can be a little janky but very fun, worth 1.99 for some mixed reality fun. There isn't enough mixed reality games! Love the direction this game is heading.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this review. Seriously made my day. I hope to make the game better for you every week!

reviews.review_description: The concept is promising tactical assault like renders plus mixed reality not fun but it's okay for the price. I hope the devs improve and optimize it further, for example it won't recognize my newly saved spatial data when I am in a different place. Why isn't this even searchable on the Meta Store?

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for the review!  I get it, it is brand new but I'm dedicated for sure. The next stage is a map builder which will make things 100% better.  I will look into not the spatial data issue but that isn't something that the game even attempts to do right now.  It does ask to use spatial data on every boot up.  Either way thanks for playing and I promise to improve the game.

reviews.review_description: This game is not ready for launching to be honest. When I grabbed my gun I discovered after a minute or two it wasn’t in my hand anymore but floating in a space I could not reach it. This happened a couple of time and also in case of a door I opened ( it was literally gone from the wall it was build on). Still had a good time for 2,99. Can only hope for updates soon cause the game need it desperately.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to play and review the game. I agree with you, it needs a lot of work.  I had to take a summer break but I'm back at it. Hopefully I can get you to play it again one day!

reviews.review_title: A Wild MR Shooter Built on Vibes and AI
reviews.review_description:
Big respect to the dev for being completely transparent:

    “CLICK CLACK MR is the first mixed reality shooter built with AI by a dev who doesn’t know how to code. 100% vibes. 10,000% fun. Zero programming. Just AI, Unity, and a wild dream.”

And honestly? That wild dream works. Click Clack is chaotic fun in the best way — a goofy, fast-paced MR shooter that doesn’t take itself too seriously, yet still delivers a surprisingly engaging experience. It’s priced perfectly for what it offers: a slice of creative energy you can jump into for some quick laughs and fast action.

The potential here is real. With community feedback and continued development, this could evolve into something even more unique and addictive. I’m definitely rooting for it — and I hope the dev keeps iterating and sharing the journey with us.

Worth checking out if you're into experimental MR, quick fun, and supporting indie creativity driven by AI tools.

developer_response.body: Thank you!!!! Yes this is my passion project and it's not even where I want it yet but it was fun for me to play so I figured it would be fun for others. Cheers and thanks for playing and your feedback.
